text:
no such obligations;
and Woodrow Wilson is a good example, for he was swept
along by destiny himself being never sensitive to anything of
that kind. The sixth house, therefore, on the analogy of the
twelfth points to the relationship that is hidden or limited -
servant, relations, debts, etc. It implies the service he is to
render to his own affairs by way of work or sickness. Wilson
had a planet in the sixth, as a result of which he always failed
in adjusting himself to others around him. The eighth, as
usual, speaks of the expanding possibilities of these relations,
legacies and the means. It speaks of the development of char
acter arising out of human relations, and of death, which is
the final chapter in this regeneration. The help one can render
and the help one receives are given out here. Wilson and the
Queen have planets here, and the part played by reciprocal
activity in their lives is too well-known.

Directions: Limitations
Prognostication
